title: Aane Wala Pal Jaane Wala Hai

lines.lyrics: ek baar waqt se lamha gira kahi
waha dastan mili lamha kahi nahi
thoda sa hasa ke thoda sa rula ke

description: This iconic philosophical ballad captures the fleeting nature of time and love with profound simplicity. Composed by R.D. Burman in the melancholic yet serene Raag Kirwani, Kishore Kumar's soulful rendition is beautifully complemented by Gulzar's poetic genius and a minimalist acoustic arrangement.
trivia: Kishore Kumar won the Filmfare Award for Best Male Playback Singer for this song, while Gulzar won the Filmfare Award for Best Lyricist.
The song features a remarkably minimalist arrangement dominated by an acoustic guitar and a haunting flute, showcasing R.D. Burman's versatility beyond his signature upbeat, brass-heavy compositions.

Raag Kirwani, the classical foundation of this song, is a Carnatic import into Hindustani music that resembles the Western harmonic minor scale, making it perfect for evoking deep nostalgia and bittersweet emotions.
The opening acoustic guitar arpeggio is one of the most recognized intros in Indian cinema history, instantly setting the song's contemplative and reflective mood.

meaning.coreTheme: This philosophical gem from 'Golmaal' reflects on the transient nature of time and the importance of living fully in the present moment. It encourages listeners to cherish each passing second, as once gone, it can never be reclaimed. The song serves as a gentle reminder to embrace life's experiences rather than dwelling on the past or constantly anticipating the future.
meaning.lyricSymbolism: Gulzar's lyrics beautifully personify time, referring to 'aane wala pal' (the coming moment) as something that is 'jaane wala hai' (going to pass), emphasizing its fleeting existence. The 'parde pe yaadon ke' (on the screen of memories) metaphorically describes how past moments are replayed in our minds, highlighting the bittersweet nature of nostalgia. The simplicity of the language belies the profound message, using everyday imagery to convey deep existential truths about impermanence and the value of now.
